title: Frontend form submission handler without CSRF validation
description: A frontend action processes a form submit and performs a state-changing operation without CSRF token validation. Admin actions are excluded because the CMSMS admin framework handles CSRF protection automatically.
explanation: Frontend state-changing operations triggered by form submission without CSRF token validation are vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery. Admin actions are excluded because CMSMS handles admin CSRF protection at the framework level. For frontend forms, the CMSMSExt module offers a robust CSRF solution.
fix: Install the CMSMSExt module. In templates, add {xt_form_csrf} inside your form: {form_start}{xt_form_csrf}. In the action handler, validate with: if(!\xt_utils::valid_form_csrf()) { throw new Exception($this->Lang('error_security')); }. This is recommended best practice but not mandatory.
